newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Latest fundamentals are from the April 29 Q1 print (Q1 sales EUR 202M, slightly ahead; H1 guided flat y/y, Q2 ~EUR 208M below consensus; H2 sales expected ~+10% sequential to ~EUR 450M). Recent analyst actions (Bernstein Buy May 28, JPMorgan PT to EUR 67 May 1) predate the window. Only routine items since: refreshed investor materials and the MLX91299 SiC snubber product note. Today's -3.3% (after -4.6% on June 5) looks like flow-driven profit-taking off the +34.8% YTD run, not a fundamental event; next catalyst is Q2 results on July 29.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - MELE.BR quote and news
Investing.com - Melexis Q1 sales ahead, maintains H1 guidance
ad-hoc-news - Melexis refreshed investor materials (automotive sensors focus)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already pushed the name down to reconcile the adjusted-score rank; with zero new evidence since June 6, moving again would be churn. The two-day ~8% flow-driven pullback modestly de-crowds the +34.8% YTD run, justifying a small positive drift but not a rank change on a strict latent-AI-nodes screen where the pathway explicitly stops short of AI accelerators.
verdicts.bull: Best-in-class automotive magnetic/current/position sensor franchise with H2 guided ~+10% sequential to ~EUR 450M; current-sensing and thermal-monitoring ICs have a plausible latent pathway into data-center power-delivery and cooling instrumentation.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis unchanged since the April Q1 print; today's -3.3% is profit-taking, not news. The name remains a weak-evidence, low-rank entry on a strict AI-nodes list, fairly placed at 84.
verdicts.bear: The page's own thesis concedes Melexis is not an AI-accelerator node; +34.8% YTD rerating was driven by auto-cycle recovery hopes, so any China-auto demand slip or H2 guidance cut at the July 29 print removes the only support without adding AI optionality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results on July 29 confirming the guided H2 sequential acceleration, or a concrete design win placing Melexis current/thermal sensors into data-center power infrastructure.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cautious and uneven automotive demand (especially China) forcing an H2 guidance cut, compressing a stock that has already rerated +34.8% YTD on a non-AI thesis.
